name: Willapa Bay Oysters Courvoisier
recipeInstructions:
1. Finely chop the herbs and sauté them in 2 tablespoons of butter until wilted. Set aside.
2. In a medium saucepan, combine the cognac with the lemon peel and gently simmer until reduced to about 3 tablespoons. Remove the lemon peel and discard it.
3. Add 8 tablespoons of butter and 12 teaspoons of green peppercorns. Simmer to combine the flavors. Set aside.
4. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. If using oysters in their shells, shuck them. Place the deeper half of each shell in a baking pan. To prevent the oysters from sliding, use either rock salt or crinkled foil as a base.
6. If oysters are not available in their shells, prepare the recipe in porcelain dishes or ramekins.
7. Add about 2 teaspoons of herb mixture to each shell or spread it over the bottom of each dish. Sprinkle with grated cheese (about 1 teaspoon per shell).
8. Place the oysters on the cheese and herb beds and glaze each with the Courvoisier green peppercorn mixture.
9. Bake for about 6 to 8 minutes.
